description_long: This is a traditional Japanese torii gate, typically found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. The structure is composed of two tall cylindrical red pillars supporting two horizontal crossbars, with the upper crossbar extending outward on both sides. A rectangular wooden plaque is suspended in the center, and decorative shide (zigzag paper streamers) hang from the crossbars, signifying its sacred nature. The red paint on the pillars shows some weathering, adding to its historical character. The overall design is simple yet elegant, emphasizing symmetry and cultural symbolism.
description: A weathered red Japanese torii gate with cylindrical pillars, horizontal crossbars, a central wooden plaque, and hanging shide streamers.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the cylindrical pillar and the vertical profile of the torii gate.
description_view_1: The front view displays both red pillars, the wide crossbars, central wooden plaque, and hanging shide streamers.
description_view_2: Another narrow side view, similar to view 0, showing the vertical profile and pillar.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the wooden plaque and the symmetrical arrangement of the pillars and crossbars.
